values.content:
=Based on this week's content performance data, provide 3 actionable recommendations for improving our social media strategy. **Overall Stats:** - Total Posts: {{$json.totalPosts}} ({{$json.postsWithImpressions}} with impression data, {{$json.postsWithoutImpressions}} without) - Avg Engagement Rate (WES): {{$json.avgEngagementRate}}% **Platform Rankings:** {{$json.platformRankings.map(p => `${p.name}: ${p.avgEngagementRate}% (${p.totalPosts} posts, ${p.totalImpressions} impressions)`).join('\n')}} **Best Performing Post ({{$json.bestPost ? $json.bestPost.rate.toFixed(2) : 'N/A'}}%):** {{$json.bestPost ? $json.bestPost.text : 'No posts with impression data this week'}} **Worst Performing Post ({{$json.worstPost ? $json.worstPost.rate.toFixed(2) : 'N/A'}}%):** {{$json.worstPost ? $json.worstPost.text : 'No posts with impression data this week'}} CRITICAL OUTPUT FORMATTING: Return your complete response in clean, semantic HTML format. Use ONLY

for section titles,

for body text, for emphasis, and

    /
  • for action items. Do NOT wrap the response in markdown code blocks. Begin output directly with the first HTML tag.

assignments.name: aiInsights
assignments.value:
={{ $('AI Strategy Insights').first().json.error ? '

AI insights unavailable this week: ' + $('AI Strategy Insights').first().json.error.message + '

' : ($('AI Strategy Insights').first().json.message.content || '').replace(/```html|```/g, '') }}

parameters.html:
=

📊 Weekly Content Performance Report

Period: {{$json.weekStart}} to {{$json.weekEnd}}

={{ $json.sheetErrorCount > 0 ? '
⚠️ Warning: ' + $json.sheetErrorCount + ' post(s) failed to log to Google Sheets.
Errors: ' + ($json.sheetErrors || []).join(', ') + '
' : '' }}

Key Metrics

Total Posts {{$json.totalPosts}}
Posts with impression data {{$json.postsWithImpressions}} / {{$json.totalPosts}}
Total Impressions {{Number($json.totalImpressions).toLocaleString()}}
Avg Engagement Rate (WES) {{$json.avgEngagementRate}}%

💡 AI Strategy Recommendations

{{$json.aiInsights}}
parameters.subject: =Weekly Content Performance Report ({{$json.weekStart}} - {{$json.weekEnd}}){{$json.sheetErrorCount > 0 ? ' ⚠️ Sheet errors' : ''}}

parameters.content:
### Multi-Platform Data Fetch
Pulls the last 7 days of posts from LinkedIn, X (Twitter), and Instagram in
parallel, merges LinkedIn and X first, then combines all three streams into
a single unified feed for downstream processing.

### Normalization & Validation
Detects the platform of each post, extracts engagement metrics into a unified schema, filters out posts older than 7 days, and drops any empty items before passing clean data to both downstream branches.

### Trigger & Weight Config
Fires every Monday at 9 AM and sets the engagement weights used across the entire pipeline: likes (1), comments (3), shares (5), saves (4).

### WES Calculation & AI Insights
Calculates the Weighted Engagement Score for each post and aggregates platform rankings, best and worst performers, and overall stats. GPT-4o then generates 3 actionable strategy recommendations based on the week's performance data.

### Raw Post Logging
Iterates over normalized posts in batches of 50 and appends each one to a Google Sheets log with date, platform, content preview, impressions, engagement score, and URL. Sheet write errors are captured without crashing the loop.

### Report Assembly & Delivery
Waits for both the Sheets logging and AI insights branches to complete, merges all analytics data and AI output into a single payload, and sends a formatted HTML email report with key metrics, platform rankings, and strategy recommendations.

## Weekly Social Media Analytics: WES Scoring, AI Insights, and Automated Email Report

### Cross-Platform Content Performance Reporting via LinkedIn, X, and Instagram

### How it works
This workflow runs every Monday at 9 AM, pulls the last 7 days of posts from LinkedIn, X (Twitter), and Instagram, calculates a Weighted Engagement Score for each post, generates AI strategy recommendations with GPT-4o, logs raw data to Google Sheets, and delivers a formatted HTML report by email.

### Setup steps
- [ ] Set the LINKEDIN_OWNER_URN environment variable to your LinkedIn URN
      (format: urn:li:person:XXXXXXXX or urn:li:organization:XXXXXXXX).
- [ ] Set the TWITTER_SEARCH_QUERY environment variable to the search query
      or handle you want to monitor (e.g. from:yourusername).
- [ ] Set the ANALYTICS_SPREADSHEET_ID environment variable to your Google
      Sheets document ID where raw posts should be logged.
- [ ] Connect your LinkedIn OAuth2 credential to the Get LinkedIn Posts node.
- [ ] Connect your X (Twitter) credential to the Search Tweets node.
- [ ] Connect your Instagram credential to the Get media node.
- [ ] Connect your OpenAI credential to the AI Strategy Insights node.
- [ ] Connect your Google Sheets credential to the Log Raw Posts to Sheets node.
- [ ] Update the fromEmail and toEmail values in the Email Weekly Report node
      to match your sender and recipient addresses.
- [ ] Adjust the engagement weights in the WES Config node to reflect your
      team's priorities. Default: likes 1, comments 3, shares 5, saves 4.

### Required credentials
- **LinkedIn OAuth2** (post fetch)
- **X (Twitter) OAuth2** (tweet search)
- **Instagram** (media fetch)
- **OpenAI API** (strategy recommendations via GPT-4o)
- **Google Sheets** (raw post logging)
- **SMTP / email** (weekly report delivery)

### Environment variables required
`LINKEDIN_OWNER_URN` · `TWITTER_SEARCH_QUERY` · `ANALYTICS_SPREADSHEET_ID`

### Notes
WES (Weighted Engagement Score) is calculated as:
`(likes × 1) + (comments × 3) + (shares × 5) + (saves × 4) / impressions × 100`
Posts without impression data are excluded from the engagement rate calculation
but still counted in total post volume. Adjust weights in the WES Config node.
